mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 6.5%, or signal loses predictive power with t-stat below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicate robust consumer spending without inflationary pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during easing periods, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference from baseline with p-value above 0.05 when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during sustained payroll expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances following a greater than 5% housing starts decline, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stationary or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive forward returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over single-series baselines in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2 percent during a post-shock recovery phase confirms sustained economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive accuracy with Rank IC below 0.02 during recovery phases, or forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of quarters with GDP growth above 2%.
